goal: 修复 CI 中 TestPropertySupersessionRanking 失败

summary.title: Supersession ranking is enforced after relevance sort
summary.what: Changed context retrieval ranking so supersession is applied as a hard ordering constraint after relevance sorting. The helper now repeatedly moves each superseder directly above the superseded intent, which handles supersession chains. Added a focused regression test for old -> mid -> new chains.
summary.why: GitHub CI found a rapid seed where TestPropertySupersessionRanking failed: a supersession chain could still leave a superseded intent ranked above its superseder because the old implementation only adjusted scores before the final sort.
summary.user_goal: 最小路径修复 CI 中 TestPropertySupersessionRanking 失败

decisions.chose: Sort by relevance first, then enforce supersession ordering by moving the superseder ahead of the superseded intent.
decisions.rationale: Supersession is a semantic hard constraint, not just another relevance score contribution; enforcing after sort prevents chains from being undone by final score ordering.

decisions.chose: Add a focused chain test around enforceSupersessionRanking rather than broadening unrelated retrieval tests.
decisions.rationale: The CI failure was a specific chain-ordering counterexample; the narrow test captures that root cause with minimal fixture cost.

rejected.alternative: Only lower the superseded score further
rejected.reason: Score pinning remains fragile for chains because later pair adjustments can invalidate earlier ordering.

rejected.alternative: Relax or remove the property test
rejected.reason: The property encodes a real retrieval contract: replacements must be shown before replaced intents when both are relevant.

risks: The ordering helper assumes supersession links form an acyclic replacement graph. If corrupted data introduced cycles, the bounded pass count prevents infinite loops but the resulting order would be best-effort.

Local full internal/engine test was not used as completion evidence because it hung on an unrelated reconcile test under the local toolchain; the CI-failing rapid seed and race-mode property path were verified directly.

anti_patterns.what: Encoding supersession ordering solely by adjusting relevance scores before sorting
anti_patterns.why: Score pinning can be invalidated by supersession chains; replacements must rank above replaced intents as a hard post-sort constraint.

followups: If retrieval ranking grows more hard constraints, consider isolating them into an explicit topological ordering layer after relevance scoring.

behavioral_changes: context retrieval now enforces superseder-before-superseded ordering after relevance sorting
supersession chains such as old -> mid -> new now return new before mid before old when all are relevant
